sector: Technology — Photonics / Optical Interconnect (AI scale-out + scale-up)
bull_thesis: Coherent is the broadest vertically-integrated photonics platform in the AI optical-interconnect bottleneck — the physical layer every hyperscaler and sovereign-AI capex dollar must traverse. The business has been transformed: net leverage cut to 0.5x (from 2.1x a year ago), non-GAAP gross margin expanded in 7 of the last 8 quarters (+530bp cumulative to 39.6%, with a >42% Investor-Day target still owed), and Datacenter & Communications is now 75% of revenue growing +40% YoY (data center +37% YoY, communications/scale-across +60% YoY). FQ3-FY26 revenue $1.806B (+21% GAAP / +27% pro forma YoY, +9% QoQ), non-GAAP EPS $1.41 (+55% YoY), op margin 20.3%. The FQ4 guide ($1.91–2.05B, EPS $1.52–1.72) implies ACCELERATING growth, and management explicitly guides FY27 growth > FY26. The company is supply-constrained, not demand-constrained — record backlog, orders into CY2028, LTAs to end of decade with customer upfront CapEx + minimum-demand commitments. The master variable is the 6-inch indium-phosphide ramp (Sherman TX / Sweden / Zurich): >4x devices at <½ the cost, doubling internal InP capacity by end-CY26 (a quarter early) and quadrupling by end-CY27 — simultaneously the volume unlock AND the gross-margin lever. On top of the core 800G/1.6T transceiver ramp (1.6T ramping faster than expected, ASP step-up vs 800G), four stacked growth vectors layer in: OCS (>$4B TAM, doubled at OFC, bottleneck just cleared), CPO/co-packaged optics (>$15B incremental TAM, anchored by the March-2026 NVIDIA partnership — $2B equity + multi-year supply agreement through end of decade, scale-out revenue H2-CY26, scale-up H2-CY27), multi-rail DCI (>$2B TAM, H1-CY27) and AI-thermal solutions (Thermadite/thermoelectric, H2-CY27). Pricing power is a quiet tailwind ('pricing optimization' a recurring GM driver in a supply-short market). NVDA's $2B stake validates the platform and funds the capex. New operator management (Anderson ex-Lattice / Luther) is under-promising and over-delivering. Conditions: AI-capex durability holds; InP ramp stays on schedule; CPO/OCS revenue materializes on the H2-CY26/CY27 timeline; GM continues toward >42%.
bear_thesis: The business is excellent; the price is the entire problem. At ~$389/share, ~$76B market cap, ~52x forward P/E and ~9–10x EV/sales, the stock has run +171% in 12 months (from a $77.84 low to a $440 high) and sits near all-time highs — pricing in a vast amount of CPO/OCS/multi-rail/thermal optionality that generates ESSENTIALLY ZERO revenue today (CPO starts H2-CY26; multi-rail/thermal CY27). Free cash flow is deeply negative — FQ3 FCF −$383M, TTM FCF negative, capex $290M and RISING — so the equity is valued as a cash machine it is not yet. The >42% gross-margin target is unmet (39.6%), and the sharpest analyst line (Barclays/Jefferies) — 'why isn't there more incremental GM fall-through given 6-inch InP at half the cost?' — was conceded: the margin upside is mostly AHEAD, not banked, i.e. execution-dependent. Returns are unimpressive today (ROIC ~4%, ROE ~5% TTM), depressed by the growth-capex phase and a heavy acquired-intangible base (~43% of assets). Management deflected the head-to-head OCS/CPO competitive comparison vs Lumentum — COHR's actual share of the next-gen layers is UNPROVEN. The entire thesis rides a single macro factor — AI-capex durability — the same wall under every other name in the scale-out basket, so it adds zero resolution-driver independence and de-grosses with the cohort on any hyperscaler capex scare. Technology-transition risk is live (copper persistence in scale-up, rival CPO architectures, the SiPho-vs-EML-vs-VCSEL roadmap fork). Confirmation of the bear: an InP-ramp slip (which would hit growth AND margin simultaneously), a CPO/OCS revenue timeline push, or a single AI-capex guide-down from a hyperscaler. This is a great company at a demanding price into a concentrated basket — the catalyst (NVDA/CPO) has already largely FIRED into the +171% move.
sop_bull: $470–500/share. Whole-company forward-earnings approach on the AI-optical franchise (segment SoP not warranted — Datacenter & Communications is 75% of revenue and the value driver; Industrial is a small declining tail). Drivers: FQ4-FY26 non-GAAP EPS guide midpoint $1.62 annualizes to a ~$6.5 exit run-rate; with FY27 growth > FY26 (accelerating top line + GM marching to >42% + operating leverage), FY27 non-GAAP EPS reaches ~$9.0–9.5. Bull multiple ~50x on $9.25 = ~$460, plus CPO/OCS/multi-rail/thermal optionality (NVDA-anchored, >$15B CPO TAM) carried as a ~$10–40 option layer → ~$470–500. Cross-check: ~10x EV/sales on a ~$8–8.5B FY27 revenue run-rate ≈ $80–85B EV → ~$76B+ equity ≈ $415–435, with the optionality premium taking it to ~$480. Net cash is modest (net debt $1.83B) so no balance-sheet kicker; the $2B NVDA cash is already in the cash line and funds capex.
sop_bear: $230–270/share. De-rate to a market-respecting growth multiple as FCF stays negative and the priced-in optionality is discounted. FY27 non-GAAP EPS held to ~$7.5 (slower CPO/OCS ramp, GM stalls ~40% short of target) at a ~32–35x multiple = ~$240–265. Cross-check: ~5–6x EV/sales on ~$7.5B forward revenue ≈ $40–45B EV ≈ ~$200–230/share, plus a partial CPO-credit to ~$255. This is where the stock trades if AI-capex durability is questioned, the InP ramp slips, or the cohort de-rates — roughly a 35–40% drawdown from spot, i.e. back toward the 200-day (~$224) plus the franchise premium.
sop_breakdown: Whole-company intrinsic on the AI-optical franchise. Base case: FY27 non-GAAP EPS ~$8.5 (accelerating revenue + GM grinding from 39.6% toward, but not fully to, the >42% target + operating leverage) at a ~40x multiple (premium to the optical/AI-infra cohort for breadth, NVDA anchor, and demonstrated margin trajectory, but a discount to the bull's 50x to reflect negative FCF and single-factor macro dependence) = ~$340. Add a modest ~$5–15/share for CPO/OCS/multi-rail/thermal optionality net of execution risk (NVDA-anchored but pre-revenue) → ~$345–355. EV/sales cross-check: ~8x on a ~$8.3B FY27 revenue run-rate ≈ $66B EV ≈ ~$345/share equity. Net debt $1.83B is immaterial to the per-share math. Industrial segment (~25% of revenue, declining) contributes <$30/share at a mid-teens multiple and is a drag, not a sum-of-parts unlock. Base anchor ~$345, i.e. roughly in line with spot (~$389) to a modest ~11% downside — the franchise quality is real but the stock has already paid forward most of CY26-27.
risk_reward: Roughly symmetric-to-unfavorable from spot. Spot ~$389. Upside-to-bull ~$485 = +25%. Downside-to-bear ~$250 = −36%. Downside-to-base ~$345 = −11%. Risk/reward ≈ 0.7:1 against (≈25% up vs ≈36% down to the bear case). The asymmetry has compressed because the +171% move and the NVDA/CPO catalyst have already largely fired — you are no longer being paid asymmetrically to own the optionality.
catalyst_status: FIRED (primary) + SOFT_EXTENDED (secondary). The load-bearing catalyst — the NVIDIA strategic partnership ($2B equity investment + multi-year CPO supply agreement through end of decade) — was ANNOUNCED March 2, 2026 and is the proximate driver of the +171%/12-month, near-all-time-high move; the re-rate is largely spent (FIRED). The forward catalysts (CPO scale-out revenue H2-CY26, scale-up H2-CY27, OCS ramp, multi-rail H1-CY27, thermal H2-CY27, InP doubling by end-CY26) are real, dated, and management-committed but are CAPACITY-RAMP / future-revenue milestones gated on the single shared AI-capex factor and on the company's own execution — SOFT_EXTENDED in the sense that they are serially forward-dated and not yet revenue-proven (CPO/OCS share vs Lumentum unquantified, mgmt deflected the comparison). Hard-dated near-term forcing function = the FQ4-FY26 print (~early Aug 2026) which must confirm the accelerating-growth + GM-toward-42% trajectory. Verified live June 2026.
peer_comps_note: True scale-out OPTICAL peers (live multiples verified June 2026): Lumentum (LITE) ~$850, ~$66B mcap — the most direct OCS/CPO competitor and the 'perceived gap' management was asked about; LITE screens at a far richer ~27x EV/sales TTM (depressed trailing sales recovering), reflecting an even more aggressive AI-optical re-rate. Fabrinet (FN) ~$574, ~$20.6B — the contract-manufacturing optical assembler (NVDA/datacom transceiver exposure), lower-multiple, lower-margin, the 'value' end of the cohort. Credo (CRDO) ~$272, ~$50.7B — the AECs/SerDes + SiPho-PIC play, the hyper-growth/hyper-multiple name (stock near 52-wk high). Adjacent: Marvell (MRVL) ~$311 (custom silicon + optical DSP), Nvidia (NVDA) ~$211 (the customer/sponsor). WHERE COHR RANKS: on VALUATION it is mid-cohort — ~9–10x EV/sales / ~52x fwd P/E is RICH absolutely but CHEAPER than LITE (~27x EV/sales) and CRDO, and richer than FN. On GROWTH, COHR's +27% pro-forma YoY is solid but below CRDO's hyper-growth; accelerating. On MARGINS, COHR's 39.6% non-GAAP GM / 20.3% op margin is mid-pack — better than FN (assembler), below CRDO/LITE peak-cycle. On RETURNS, COHR's ROIC ~4% / ROE ~5% is the WEAKEST in the cohort (growth-capex + intangible drag) — a genuine red flag vs LITE's ~31% ROE. On AI-CAPEX EXPOSURE, COHR has the BROADEST platform exposure (transceivers + OCS + CPO + DCI + thermal) and the marquee NVDA $2B anchor — arguably the highest-quality scale-out optionality, which is precisely why it is not cheap.
role_in_scaleout: Coherent is the broadest vertically-integrated photonics-materials supplier in the AI optical-interconnect bottleneck — it makes the indium-phosphide lasers (EML/CW), silicon-photonics, VCSELs, fiber, lens arrays, isolators and TECs that go into 800G/1.6T transceivers, OCS switches, DCI/multi-rail systems and NVIDIA's co-packaged-optics — i.e. the physical layer carrying every bit between and inside AI clusters; demand is highly durable so long as AI-capex and bandwidth-per-GPU keep scaling, with the InP supply constraint giving it pricing power, though the durability is fully levered to the single AI-capex factor.
consensus_delta: The street is NOT looking the wrong way on Coherent — coverage is heavy (21+ analysts, consensus 'Buy', PT ~$384 essentially AT spot), and the AI-optical narrative is fully discovered after a +171%/12-month run to near all-time highs. The expectations-arbitrage edge is therefore THIN, and it cuts in an unusual direction: the bull mispricing the street under-models is COHR's PRICING POWER and the 6-inch-InP MARGIN lever (GM mostly ahead, not banked) plus the breadth of stacked growth vectors (multi-rail + thermal are barely in models); the bear mispricing is that the street is capitalizing CPO/OCS/multi-rail/thermal optionality at ~52x fwd P/E / ~9–10x EV/sales BEFORE it earns a dollar, while FCF is negative (−$383M FQ3) and ROIC is ~4%. The incorrect street assumption most worth flagging: that the NVDA partnership + CPO TAM is incremental upside still to be priced — it has ALREADY fired into the move; from here the consensus and the print are roughly aligned, so there is no fat expectations gap to arbitrage. This is a momentum/quality name where the price already reflects the good news, not a contrarian re-rate.
valley_of_death: Near-term (3–9 month) risks are valuation/positioning, not solvency — the balance sheet is healthy (0.5x leverage, $3B cash incl. NVDA's $2B, no maturity wall). (1) FREE-CASH-FLOW HOLE: capex is $290M and guided to rise sequentially while FCF runs negative — a high-beta (β~2.05), near-all-time-high stock that burns cash is acutely vulnerable to any multiple compression. (2) FQ4-FY26 PRINT (~early Aug 2026) is the binary: it must confirm accelerating growth AND GM toward 42% — any GM stall (the conceded 'fall-through' question) or guide-down de-rates a 52x stock hard. (3) AI-CAPEX SCARE: per CATALYST_WATCH_REGIME §5, AI-capex is ~40% of 2026 S&P EPS growth and the single dominant factor under the tape; a hyperscaler capex guide-down would de-gross the entire optical cohort (LITE/CRDO/FN/COHR) together — COHR has zero independence from this. (4) INP-RAMP SLIP would hit growth AND margin simultaneously. (5) Cohort/factor unwind: BofA FMS cash at a 3.9% sell-signal with Mag-7 ~30% of the index = little dry powder to absorb an AI-capex shock. The macro tape (complacent melt-up over a live oil/inflation tail, Fed on hold) raises the stakes on a name priced for perfection.
positioning_washout: No dividend-cut / yield-base washout mechanism applies (de-minimis dividend, growth shareholder base). The relevant mechanical-flow dynamic is the opposite of a value washout: COHR is a momentum/Mag-7-adjacent AI-optical name with high beta (~2.05) near all-time highs, +171% in 12 months — it is OWNED by momentum, growth and passive/AI-thematic flows, not contrarian value. The forced-selling risk is therefore a DE-GROSSING/momentum-reversal washout: on any AI-capex scare or factor rotation, the same crowded flows that drove the +171% reverse mechanically and the whole optical cohort sells together (no independence). There is no clean capitulation flush to BUY into here as the entry — if anything, the disciplined posture is to WAIT for exactly such a cohort de-gross to reset the entry, since you are currently buying near the high into crowded positioning.
forcing_function: Hard dates: (1) FQ4-FY26 earnings ~early August 2026 — the binary that confirms or breaks the accelerating-growth + GM-toward-42% trajectory and the first read on OCS/CPO revenue actually starting. (2) H2-CY26 — initial scale-out CPO revenue must begin (the NVDA-anchored milestone); a slip is a thesis dent. (3) End-CY26 — InP internal capacity doubling milestone (guided a quarter early). (4) H1-CY27 — multi-rail first revenue; H2-CY27 — scale-up CPO + thermal first revenue. The most consequential near-term forcing function is the August print: it is where the market re-tests whether the +171% move is justified by accelerating fundamentals or has front-run them.
moderator_conclusion: Coherent is one of the highest-QUALITY franchises in the scale-out OPTICAL basket — genuinely transformed (net leverage 0.5x from 2.1x), margin-expanding (7 of 8 quarters, +530bp), demand-saturated (record backlog, orders into CY28, NVDA $2B anchor), under operator management that under-promises and over-delivers, with the broadest vertically-integrated photonics platform in the AI-interconnect bottleneck and real stacked optionality (OCS/CPO/multi-rail/thermal). The Interrogator's STRENGTHENING / CREDIBILITY-4 read is fully earned. But applying the regime (CATALYST_WATCH §3/§5): the load-bearing catalyst — the NVIDIA CPO partnership — has ALREADY FIRED into a +171%/12-month, near-all-time-high move, so this is no longer an asymmetric special-sit; it is a momentum/quality name where the optionality is largely priced. At ~52x fwd P/E / ~9–10x EV/sales with NEGATIVE free cash flow and ROIC ~4%, the SoP base (~$345) sits ~11% BELOW spot (~$389), with bull ~$485 (+25%) vs bear ~$250 (−36%) — a roughly 0.7:1 risk/reward AGAINST. Critically, §4/§5 flag that AI-capex is the single dominant factor under the tape (~40% of 2026 EPS growth) and COHR adds ZERO resolution-driver independence to a basket already concentrated on that exact wall — a hyperscaler capex scare de-grosses the whole cohort together. The discipline call is B — WATCHLIST FOR CAPITULATION: own the franchise, but wait for a cohort-wide AI-capex de-gross / momentum-reversal flush (or a GM-disappointing print) to reset the entry below ~$300, rather than paying near the all-time high for priced-in, single-factor optionality. Conviction 3. value_conviction 3: stripping the catalyst overlay, the pure-value case is also a 3 — the SoP fair value is roughly AT spot (no margin of safety), and while the moat is widening and the secular threat manageable (so NOT a value-trap cap), the rich multiple on negative FCF + weak current returns caps the standalone value enthusiasm at neutral. Great company, demanding price, wrong moment to chase.
interrogator_dossier:
# COHR — Coherent Corp. | Forensic Dossier (Interrogator)

*Scale-out Basket A (OPTICAL). Source: COHR FQ3-FY2026 earnings call (May 6, 2026) + FMP fundamentals + live web verification, June 2026. Fiscal year ends ~June; FQ3 FY26 = quarter ended March 31, 2026.*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC
A clean two-act transformation under new operator management (Jim Anderson, ex-Lattice CEO; CFO Sherri Luther). Act 1 (FY25): deleveraging, portfolio pruning (sold Aerospace & Defense FQ1-FY26, sold Munich product division FQ3 at below-corporate GM), margin repair, >42% non-GAAP GM Investor-Day target. Act 2 (FY26, current): decisive pivot to AI data-center optical infrastructure as a multi-year secular growth engine — Datacenter & Communications now 75% of revenue, +40% YoY. Stacked growth vectors introduced and layered: 800G (shipping), 1.6T (ramping faster than expected), OCS (>$4B TAM, doubled at OFC, bottleneck cleared), CPO (>$15B TAM, NVIDIA partnership: $2B equity + supply agreement through end of decade, scale-out H2-CY26, scale-up H2-CY27), multi-rail DCI (>$2B TAM, H1-CY27), thermal/Thermadite (H2-CY27). Industrial soft but retained and re-cast as AI-thermal optionality. Master enabler woven through every answer: the 6-inch indium-phosphide capacity ramp (Sherman TX / Sweden / Zurich) — both volume unlock and gross-margin lever.

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS
Revenue $1.806B +21% GAAP/+27% pro forma YoY, +9% QoQ — acceleration TRUE and divestiture-adjusted honestly. Non-GAAP GM 39.6% +57bp QoQ/+105bp YoY (7 of 8 quarters up, +530bp cumulative) TRUE but still ~250bp short of >42% target. Non-GAAP EPS $1.41 +55% YoY, op margin 20.3% — operating leverage real; GAAP EPS $0.97 (intangible amort + SBC gap, normal for a roll-up). Deleveraging to 0.5x net (from 2.1x yr-ago), $162M debt paid — the most impressive forensic data point, balance sheet transformed. Record backlog / orders into CY28 / LTAs to end of decade — PLAUSIBLE but UNQUANTIFIED (no backlog number disclosed); taken on management's word + NVDA anchor. NARRATIVE RICHER THAN PRINT: CPO/OCS/multi-rail/thermal contribute ~zero revenue today; market pays ~52x fwd P/E / ~9-10x EV/sales for future revenue. PRINT RICHER THAN APPRECIATED: deleveraging underrated; FCF deeply negative (FQ3 −$383M, capex $290M rising) but it is GROWTH capex funded by NVDA cash, not distress.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE: RISING, and specific. 'Demand remains exceptionally strong with no signs of attenuation… record backlog.' Supply-constrained-not-demand-constrained framing = strongest up-cycle tell. Offensive posture: R&D raised to 9.9% of revenue, SG&A cut to 9.4%. Dated milestone commitments (InP doubling end-CY26 a quarter early; CPO H2-CY26/H2-CY27; multi-rail H1-CY27; thermal H2-CY27). Only defensive note is Industrial softness, spun forward into thermal. No macro/AI-capex hedging — itself the risk.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY: HIGH. CFO's '7 of 8 quarters GM expansion, +530bp' is delivered, falsifiable. Q4 guide ($1.91-2.05B, GM 39-41%, EPS $1.52-1.72) implies accelerating QoQ growth consistent with the legible InP→transceiver 2-3mo latency. Under-promised on InP doubling (now a quarter early) = mild sandbagging/over-delivery. Open question: ~250bp climb to >42% GM still owed. No miss pattern since leadership change.

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS: (a) GM fall-through (Barclays/Jefferies) — 'why not more incremental GM given 6-inch InP at half cost?' — answer concedes margin upside is mostly AHEAD, execution-dependent. (b) Competitive gap vs Lumentum on OCS/CPO (Raymond James) — deflected into TAM rhetoric without quantifying COHR's own OCS/CPO revenue, a real non-answer. (c) Capacity→revenue latency (TD Cowen) — answered honestly (2-3mo device-to-transceiver lag). (d) 1.6T scale — declined to break out data-rate revenue. (e) SiPho viability at 3.2T (TD Cowen) — rebutted with OFC 400G/lane demo; live roadmap debate.

## 6. RED/GREEN FLAGS. GREEN: genuine deleveraging (0.5x from 2.1x); NVDA $2B + supply agreement; supply-constrained with record backlog + LTAs with customer upfront CapEx; margin expansion delivered with 6-inch InP cost lever mostly ahead; new mgmt over-delivering; stacked real optionality. RED: deeply negative FCF (−$383M FQ3, capex rising); valuation is the whole bear case (~$76B mcap, ~52x fwd P/E, ~9-10x EV/sales, +171%/12mo near all-time highs); >42% GM unmet (39.6%); single shared macro driver (AI-capex) = zero basket independence; Industrial shrinking; OCS/CPO competitive share unquantified.

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS: pricing power a quiet GM tailwind ('pricing optimization' recurring, ASP step-up at each data-rate transition); vertical integration as moat ('we provide all the ingredients, not just the laser' — InP, fiber, lens arrays, isolators, TECs); TAM inflation as a tell (OCS doubled, CPO >$15B 'conservative' in one quarter — bullish but also how a rich stock keeps narrative ahead of print); 'allocate InP capacity to maximum margin dollars' = disciplined Lattice-DNA capital allocation; the 6-inch InP ramp is the master variable — if it slips, growth AND margin wobble together.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT: STRENGTHENING. Genuinely transformed — deleveraged, margin-expanding, demand-saturated, NVDA-anchored, operator management on dated milestones. The single thing the director must weigh: how much AI-optical optionality is already in the price (not cheap: ~52x fwd P/E, +171%/yr, near highs; CPO/OCS/multi-rail/thermal are future revenue; burns FCF today). MOAT: NARROW trending WIDER — intangible (photonics IP + 6-inch InP process) + scale-cost (vertical BoM integration) + emerging switching-costs (LTAs, NVDA CPO design-in); returns depressed today (ROIC ~4%, ROE ~5%) by growth-capex + intangible base, but margins RISING not falling and pricing power real → widening, NOT eroding. SECULAR THREAT: MANAGEABLE / arguably a beneficiary — sits on the winning side of AI scale-out; terminal risk is AI-capex cyclicality (demand-durability, not moat-erosion) + technology-transition risk (copper persistence, rival CPO, SiPho-vs-EML fork), both manageable given laser-roadmap breadth + NVDA design-in. Not cheap, so neither fear-print nor value-trap — a high-quality growth name where the debate is entirely how much future is already paid for.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: WIDENING | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable
market_cap: $76B (~$76.2B on ~195.6M shares at ~$389; note FMP's $61.8B uses a stale ~155.5M share count pre-NVDA/equity issuance)
skeptic_kill_fact: No kill_fact. The adversarial attack failed to break the load-bearing facts: every figure verifies against primary/near-primary sources and is CURRENT (post-NVDA, post-FQ3 print May 6 2026), so there is NO stale anchor. NVDA $2B strategic investment + multi-year/multi-billion-dollar CPO purchase commitment is real and primary-confirmed (NVIDIA IR + Coherent 8-K, March 2 2026). FQ3-FY26 revenue ~$1.8B (+27% pro-forma YoY) and the Q4 guide ($1.91-2.05B, EPS $1.52-1.72) verify; net leverage 0.5x (from 2.1x), cash ~$3B incl. NVDA's $2B, capex $290M (rising), share count 195.64M all confirm. Because this is a SCALE-OUT SKEPTIC on a name whose director verdict is already B/WATCHLIST and whose thesis IS the bear (price-kill, not company-kill), the verified facts REINFORCE the bear: the stock at ~$389 (vs 52wk high $440, all-time-high $426.89 on 2026-06-02), ~52x fwd P/E, ~11.7x EV/sales (RICHER than the dossier's stated 9-10x), with TTM FCF of roughly -$538M and the NVDA/CPO catalyst already FIRED into the +171% move, is priced for perfection with zero basket independence from the single AI-capex factor. The bear case survives the kill-check.
skeptic_corrections: (1) VALUATION UNDERSTATED IN BULL DIRECTION: EV/sales is ~11.7x (stockanalysis.com, Jun 2026), not the dossier's '~9-10x' — the name is RICHER than stated, strengthening the bear, not weakening it. (2) FCF FIGURE: bear cites FQ3 FCF -$383M; the verifiable consolidated number is TTM FCF ~ -$538M on TTM OCF ~ +$140M — directionally identical (deeply negative, capex-driven) and if anything worse; the single-quarter -$383M is plausible but not separately primary-confirmed, so cite TTM. (3) 'Net debt $1.83B' is stale relative to the post-NVDA balance sheet: total debt ~$3.19B less ~$3B cash ≈ roughly net-neutral, not $1.83B net debt — immaterial to per-share math but the SoP text should not lean on a $1.83B net-debt figure.
skeptic_evidence: NVIDIA Investor Relations / press release, 2026-03-02: 'NVIDIA and Coherent Announce Strategic Partnership to Develop Optics Technology' — $2B NVIDIA investment in Coherent + non-exclusive multi-billion-dollar NVIDIA purchase commitment + future access/capacity rights, five additional CPO-tied product families (investor.nvidia.com).
Coherent Corp. Form 8-K (SEC EDGAR CIK 0000820318, accession 000119312526084366, ex-99.1), filed March 2026 — primary filing of the NVIDIA strategic investment terms.

Coherent FQ3-FY26 results (quarter ended 2026-03-31, reported 2026-05-06): record revenue ~$1.8B (+27% pro-forma YoY, +9% QoQ), Q4 guide $1.91-2.05B / EPS $1.52-1.72, net leverage 0.5x (from 2.1x), capex $290M, cash ~$3B incl. NVDA $2B — Coherent earnings call / Motley Fool & Globe and Mail transcripts, 2026-05-06.

stockanalysis.com/stocks/cohr (data as of 2026-06-18): price $389.57, market cap $76.22B, shares 195.64M, fwd P/E 52.33, EV/Sales 11.70, TTM OCF +$140.34M, TTM FCF -$538.24M; macrotrends: 52wk high $440, all-time-high close $426.89 on 2026-06-02.

scale.role: Broadest vertically-integrated photonics platform and the InP supply node for 800G/1.6T transceivers + the NVDA CPO partner ($2B equity, supply through end-of-decade); the toll every optical-interconnect dollar pays.
scale.rationale: Purest least-redundant optical CORE alongside FN: supply-constrained (orders into CY2028, LTAs to end of decade), the 6-inch InP ramp is both the volume unlock and the GM lever. This is the indispensable franchise I would actually seat in the layer — an 800-level CORE on importance + demand-durability + competitive position.
